title: Dosa
description: Dosa is a classic South Indian fermented rice and lentil crêpe known for its crisp golden exterior and soft, airy center. Made from naturally fermented batter, it is a staple breakfast and dinner dish traditionally served with sambar and a variety of chutneys.

ingredients.title: For the Dosa Batter
items: Raw rice (Sona Masoori or Ponni) – 1 cup
Whole urad dal (split & skinned black gram) – 1 cup

ingredients.title: For Cooking
items: Gingelly (Indian sesame) oil or neutral cooking oil – as required

instruction.title: 1. Soak the Ingredients
steps: Wash and soak the urad dal with fenugreek seeds in plenty of water.
Wash and soak the parboiled rice, raw rice, chana dal, and poha separately.
Soak for at least 6 hours or overnight in cooler weather.

instruction.title: 2. Grind the Batter
steps: Grind the soaked urad dal and fenugreek into a smooth, fluffy batter, adding water gradually.
Grind the soaked rice mixture into a smooth batter with a slightly coarse texture.
Combine both batters in a large bowl.

instruction.title: 3. Ferment the Batter
steps: Cover the bowl and ferment the batter for 8–12 hours in a warm place.
The batter should double in volume, develop air bubbles, and have a mild sour aroma.

instruction.title: 4. Prepare the Tawa
steps: Heat a well-seasoned cast iron or non-stick tawa over medium heat.
Sprinkle a little water; it should sizzle immediately.
Wipe the surface clean using a cloth or a cut onion dipped in oil.

instruction.title: 5. Cook the Dosa
steps: Pour a ladle of batter into the center of the tawa.
Spread it outward in a circular spiral to form a thin dosa.
Drizzle oil or ghee around the edges and lightly over the surface.
Cook until the dosa becomes crisp, golden brown, and releases easily from the pan.
For extra crispness, cook slightly longer before folding.

instruction.title: 6. Serve
steps: Fold into a triangle or roll into a cylinder.

Restaurant Style: Serve immediately while hot and crispy.
Brush with a little ghee before serving for a richer aroma.

chefTips: Urad dal creates softness, while rice provides structure and crispness.
Chana dal gives the dosa its characteristic golden color and extra crunch.
Poha improves fermentation and produces a softer interior.
In cold weather, ferment the batter inside an oven with the light turned on.
For authentic flavor, use gingelly (sesame) oil or a mixture of sesame oil and ghee.
Rub a cut onion dipped in oil over a cast iron tawa before each dosa to maintain a naturally non-stick surface.
Maintain medium-high heat throughout cooking for evenly browned, crispy dosas.
